subject: Chemistry
question_text: CrO3 , pyridine and HCl. (Pyridinium chlorochromate)
answer_explanation: This is due to the fact that— (i) In phenol, conjugation of unshared electron pair over oxygen with aromatic ring results in partial double bond character in carbon- oxygen bond. (ii) In phenol, oxygen is attached to a sp2 hybridised carbon atom while in methanol, it is attached to a sp3 hyrbidised carbon atom. The bond formed between oxygen and sp2 hybridised carbon is more stable than that formed between oxygen and sp3 hybridised carbon.
provenance_excerpt: CrO3 , pyridine and HCl. (Pyridinium chlorochromate)

subject: Chemistry
question_text: 2-Chloroethanol, due to –I effect of chlorine atom.
answer_explanation: Increasing order of acidity is ethanol < water < phenol. The phenoxide ion obtained after the removal of a proton is stabilised by resonance whereas the ethoxide ion obtained after the removal of a proton is destabilised by ‘+I’ effect of —C2H5 group. Therefore phenol is stronger acid than ethanol. On the other hand ethanol is weaker acid than water because electron releasing —C2H5 group in ethanol inreases the electron density on oxygen and hence the polarity of O—H bond in ethanol decreases which results in the decreasing acidic strength. Hence acidic strength increases in the order given above.
provenance_excerpt: 2-Chloroethanol, due to –I effect of chlorine atom.

subject: Chemistry
question_text: This is due to the fact that— (i) In phenol, conjugation of unshared electron pair over oxygen with aromatic ring results in partial double bond character in carbon- oxygen bond. (ii) In phenol, oxygen is attached to a sp2 hybridised carbon atom while in methanol, it is attached to a sp3 hyrbidised carbon atom. The bond formed between oxygen and sp2 hybridised carbon is more stable than that formed between oxygen and sp3 hybridised carbon.

subject: Chemistry
question_text: Increasing order of acidity is ethanol < water < phenol. The phenoxide ion obtained after the removal of a proton is stabilised by resonance whereas the ethoxide ion obtained after the removal of a proton is destabilised by ‘+I’ effect of —C2H5 group. Therefore phenol is stronger acid than ethanol. On the other hand ethanol is weaker acid than water because electron releasing —C2H5 group in ethanol inreases the electron density on oxygen and hence the polarity of O—H bond in ethanol decreases which results in the decreasing acidic strength. Hence acidic strength increases in the order given above. IV. Matching Type
